question:
I'm new to this codebase and need a real code-based tour before my first behavior change.

Find the central runtime flow, trace it from the public API to the code that does the work, and show the nearby code paths and tests I should read next.

graph.answer:
The central runtime flow is:

- Startup entry: [`src/server.c:main`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/src/server.c#L7924)
- Server bootstrap: [`src/server.c:initServer`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/src/server.c#L2965)
- Event loop handoff: [`src/ae.c:aeMain`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/src/ae.c#L497)
- Socket read entry: [`src/networking.c:readQueryFromClient`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/src/networking.c#L3830)
- Buffer parsing and staging: [`src/networking.c:processInputBuffer`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/src/networking.c#L3626)
- Pending-command handoff: [`src/networking.c:processCommandAndResetClient`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/src/networking.c#L3491)
- Policy gate and dispatch: [`src/server.c:processCommand`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/src/server.c#L4413)
- Actual work execution: [`src/server.c:call`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/src/server.c#L3950)

What each layer does:

- `main()` wires together config parsing, signal handling, modules, ACL, listeners, persistence loading, then enters `aeMain()`.
- `readQueryFromClient()` pulls bytes from the socket into `client->querybuf`, handles read errors, and updates per-client stats.
- `processInputBuffer()` turns raw query bytes into parsed `pendingCommand` objects, handles inline vs RESP, and decides whether work runs now or gets deferred to an I/O thread.
- `processCommandAndResetClient()` is the bridge that sets `server.current_client`, calls `processCommand()`, then restores state.
- `processCommand()` is the main policy gate:
  - command lookup
  - ACL/auth checks
  - cluster redirection
  - pause/read-only/write/OOM/loading checks
  - MULTI queuing vs immediate execution
- `call()` is where the command function pointer actually runs: `c->cmd->proc(c)`. It also centralizes timing, slowlog, replication/AOF propagation, and post-command cleanup.
- Command registration and lookup live in [`src/server.c:populateCommandTable`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/src/server.c#L3482) and [`src/server.c:lookupCommand`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/src/server.c#L3610). The generated `redisCommandTable` is the bridge from command name to `proc` function.

Nearby code paths worth reading next:

- [`src/networking.c`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/src/networking.c) around `processInlineBuffer()`, `processMultibulkBuffer()`, and `handleClientReadError()`
- [`src/server.c`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/src/server.c) around `lookupCommandLogic()`, `mustObeyClient()`, and the guard rails inside `processCommand()`
- [`src/commands/*.json`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/src/commands) if you plan to change command metadata or add/remove a command
- [`tests/unit/networking.tcl`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/tests/unit/networking.tcl)
- [`tests/unit/protocol.tcl`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/tests/unit/protocol.tcl)
- [`tests/unit/multi.tcl`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/tests/unit/multi.tcl)
- [`tests/unit/pause.tcl`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/tests/unit/pause.tcl)
- [`tests/unit/introspection.tcl`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/tests/unit/introspection.tcl)
- If your change touches auth/ACL, also read [`tests/unit/acl-v2.tcl`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/tests/unit/acl-v2.tcl)
- If it affects client lifecycle or shutdown, also read [`tests/unit/shutdown.tcl`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/tests/unit/shutdown.tcl)

The shortest useful mental model is:

- socket bytes enter in `networking.c`
- `processCommand()` decides whether the request is allowed and how it should be routed
- `call()` actually executes the command implementation

I also wrote onboarding notes for the repo. If you want to sanity-check them later, run `serena memories check` from the project root.

If you want, I can do one more pass and trace a concrete command end-to-end next, for example `SET`, `MULTI`, or `CLIENT PAUSE`.
